courses.title: Web Development
courses.subtitle: Full Stack Web Development – From Zero to Professional
courses.description: A comprehensive course covering everything you need to build modern, responsive websites and full-stack web applications. This course takes you from HTML basics to full-stack development with React, Node.js, MongoDB, Authentication, and real-time apps — preparing you for a career as a professional web developer.
courses.shortDescription: Learn HTML, CSS, Tailwind, JavaScript, React, Node.js, MongoDB, Auth, and Deployment to build real-world full-stack apps.

modules.title: Internet & Web Fundamentals
topics: HTTP vs HTTPS – Request & Response cycle
DNS and how browsers render pages
Introduction to VS Code and developer tools

modules.title: HTML5 – Structure of the Web
topics: HTML Forms – Input types, Labels, Buttons, Fieldset
Semantic HTML5 Tags (header, nav, main, section, article, aside, footer)
Accessibility in HTML (ARIA roles, alt text, tabindex)

modules.title: CSS3 – Styling the Web
topics: Box Model – Margin, Padding, Border, outline
Units – px, em, rem, %, vh, vw
CSS Positioning – static, relative, absolute, fixed, sticky

modules.title: Git & GitHub – Complete Version Control
topics: What is Version Control and why it matters
Git workflow – Working directory, Staging area, Repository
Core commands – init, add, commit, status, log
Branching and Merging – create, switch, merge, delete branches
Collaborative workflow – Forking and contributing to open source

modules.title: Tailwind CSS + Bootstrap 5 – Utility-First & Component Frameworks
topics: Tailwind installation – CDN, CLI, and Vite setup
Core utility classes – spacing, colors, typography, sizing
Responsive design with Tailwind breakpoints (sm, md, lg, xl, 2xl)
Tailwind Hover, Focus, and State variants
Tailwind Components – Cards, Navbars, Modals, Forms
Bootstrap Components – Navbar, Cards, Modals, Carousel, Accordion
Customizing Bootstrap with CSS and SCSS
Tailwind vs Bootstrap – When to use which
Building a complete responsive webpage with Tailwind

modules.title: JavaScript – Modern JS & ES6+
topics: Variables – var, let, const and scoping
Data Types – Primitive and Reference types
Operators – Arithmetic, Comparison, Logical, Nullish Coalescing (??)
Loops – for, while, do-while, for...of, for...in
Higher-Order Functions – map, filter, reduce, find, every, some
DOM Manipulation – Query, Create, Modify, Delete elements
Event Handling – Click, Input, Submit, Keyboard, Mouse events
ES6 Classes – Constructor, Inheritance, Static methods
Fetch API – GET, POST, PUT, DELETE requests
Working with REST APIs and JSON data

modules.title: React – Frontend Framework
topics: What is React and why use it – Virtual DOM
useRef – DOM access and mutable values
useContext – Context API and global state
TanStack Query (React Query) – Server state management
React Hook Form – Form handling and validation
React project structure and best practices

modules.title: Node.js – Backend Development
topics: What is Node.js – Event loop, Non-blocking I/O
Core modules – fs, path, os, events, http
File System – Read, Write, Append, Delete files
NPM – Installing, managing, and publishing packages
Building a CLI tool with Node.js

modules.title: Express.js – Web Framework
topics: What is Express and why use it
Routing – GET, POST, PUT, PATCH, DELETE
Request validation with Joi or Zod
Testing API with Postman and Thunder Client

modules.title: MongoDB & Mongoose – NoSQL Database
topics: What is NoSQL – MongoDB vs SQL databases
MongoDB CRUD – insertOne, find, updateOne, deleteOne
When to use Mongoose vs Prisma

modules.title: Authentication & Security
topics: JWT – JSON Web Tokens (Access & Refresh tokens)
Social Logins – Google, GitHub, Facebook OAuth
Clerk – Authentication platform setup, UI components, webhooks
Better-Auth – Modern auth library for Node.js/Next.js
Forgot password and Reset password flow
Security best practices – XSS, CSRF, SQL Injection, Rate limiting

modules.title: Socket.io – Real-Time Applications
topics: What are WebSockets – HTTP vs WebSocket

modules.title: Deployment & DevOps Basics
topics: Deploying frontend to Vercel and Netlify
Deploying backend to Render, Railway, and Cyclic

modules.title: Full Stack Projects
topics: Full Stack Blog App – React + Node + MongoDB + Auth
Real-time Chat App – Socket.io + React + Node
E-Commerce App – Products, Cart, Orders, Payments (Razorpay/Stripe intro)
Task Management App – Drag & Drop, Kanban board
Code review, Refactoring, and Clean code practices
Interview preparation – Common questions and concepts

projects: React E-Commerce UI with Zustand Cart
Full Stack Blog App (React + Node + MongoDB + JWT Auth)
Task Management App with Drag & Drop
Full Stack E-Commerce with Razorpay Integration

instructor.name: Hilal Ahmad
instructor.specialization: Full Stack Web Development, JavaScript, React

learningOutcomes: Build fully responsive websites using HTML, CSS, Tailwind, and Bootstrap
Write modern JavaScript with ES6+ features confidently
Build dynamic single-page applications with React
Develop REST APIs with Node.js and Express
Design and query MongoDB databases with Mongoose and Prisma

Use Git & GitHub for collaborative development
Create a professional portfolio with real-world projects

courses.title: Frontend Development
courses.subtitle: Complete Modern Frontend Development – React, Next.js & Beyond
courses.description: A deep-dive frontend course covering everything from HTML fundamentals to production-grade Next.js applications. You will master HTML5, CSS3, Tailwind CSS, modern JavaScript (ES6+), TypeScript, React, Next.js (App Router), animations, accessibility, performance optimization, and Vercel deployment — building a real portfolio along the way.
courses.shortDescription: Master HTML, CSS, Tailwind, JavaScript, TypeScript, React, and Next.js to build production-grade frontend applications.

modules.title: Internet & Web Fundamentals
topics: How the Internet works – packets, IP, TCP
HTTP vs HTTPS – Request & Response cycle
DNS resolution and how browsers render pages
Web standards, accessibility, and best practices
Dev environment setup – VS Code, extensions, terminal basics
Chrome DevTools – Elements, Console, Network, Performance tabs

modules.title: HTML5 – Structure & Semantics
topics: Links, Images, and Media embeds (audio, video, iframe)
HTML Forms – All input types, Labels, Buttons, Fieldset, Datalist
Semantic HTML5 – header, nav, main, section, article, aside, footer, figure
HTML5 APIs intro – Drag & Drop, History API
Meta tags, Open Graph, Twitter Cards
Web Accessibility (a11y) – ARIA roles, landmarks, tabindex, focus management

modules.title: CSS3 – Styling, Layout & Motion
topics: Box Model – Margin, Padding, Border, Outline
CSS Units – px, em, rem, %, vw, vh, svh, dvh, ch, clamp()
CSS Grid – Template columns/rows, named areas, subgrid
Responsive Design – Mobile-first, Media Queries, Container Queries
CSS Positioning – static, relative, absolute, fixed, sticky
Dark mode with prefers-color-scheme + CSS variables

modules.title: Git & GitHub – Complete Version Control
topics: What is Version Control and why it matters
Installing Git and initial config (name, email, editor)
Git workflow – Working directory, Staging area, Repository
Core commands – init, add, commit, status, log, diff
Branching – create, switch, rename, delete branches
Git Stash – save and restore uncommitted work
Git Rebase – interactive rebase, squashing commits
Git Reset and Revert – undo mistakes safely
Remote repos – GitHub setup, Push, Pull, Fetch, Clone
GitHub Pages for free static deployment
Forking and contributing to open source

modules.title: Tailwind CSS – Utility-First Styling
topics: Tailwind installation – CDN, CLI, Vite, PostCSS
Core utilities – spacing, sizing, colors, typography
Responsive prefixes – sm, md, lg, xl, 2xl
State variants – hover, focus, active, disabled, group, peer
Dark mode – class and media strategy
tailwind.config.js – extending theme, custom colors, fonts
Building a Navbar, Cards, Forms, Badges, Modals with Tailwind
Optimizing for production – PurgeCSS / content paths

modules.title: Bootstrap 5 – Component-First Framework
topics: Bootstrap Components – Navbar, Cards, Modals, Carousel, Accordion, Tabs, Offcanvas
Bootstrap Utility Classes – spacing, display, flex, text
Customizing Bootstrap with CSS and SCSS variables

modules.title: JavaScript – Modern JS & ES6+
topics: Variables – var, let, const – scoping and hoisting
Data Types – Primitive vs Reference types
Operators – Arithmetic, Comparison, Logical, Nullish Coalescing (??), Optional Chaining (?.)
Loops – for, while, do-while, for...of, for...in, forEach
Default Parameters, Rest Parameters, Arguments object
Higher-Order Functions – map, filter, reduce, find, findIndex, every, some, flat, flatMap
DOM Manipulation – querySelector, createElement, insertAdjacentHTML, remove
Event Handling – click, input, change, submit, keydown, mousemove
Arrays – all methods (slice, splice, sort, reverse, fill, copyWithin, at)
Objects – methods, shorthand, computed keys, Object.entries/keys/values/assign/freeze
JSON – Parse, Stringify, deep clone patterns
ES6 Classes – constructor, extends, super, static, private fields (#)
ES Modules – import, export, named, default, dynamic import()
Promise combinators – Promise.all, race, any, allSettled
Fetch API – GET, POST, PUT, PATCH, DELETE with headers
Working with REST APIs and JSON responses
Axios – HTTP client as alternative to Fetch
Web APIs – Intersection Observer, Resize Observer, MutationObserver
Browser APIs – Geolocation, Clipboard, Notifications, Web Storage
JavaScript Design Patterns – Module, Singleton, Observer, Factory

modules.title: TypeScript – Typed JavaScript
topics: What is TypeScript and why use it
Basic types – string, number, boolean, null, undefined, any, unknown, never, void
Functions – parameter types, return types, overloads
Utility Types – Partial, Required, Readonly, Pick, Omit, Record, ReturnType, Parameters
Type Guards and narrowing – typeof, instanceof, in, is
TypeScript with React – typing Props, State, Events, Refs, Context
Common TypeScript patterns and best practices

modules.title: React – Complete Frontend Framework
topics: What is React – Virtual DOM, Reconciliation, Fiber
Setting up React with Vite + TypeScript
Props – passing, receiving, typing with TypeScript
useState – state basics, functional updates, batching
Conditional Rendering – &&, ternary, early return, switch
Lists and Keys – rendering arrays correctly
useEffect – Side effects, Dependencies array, Cleanup
useRef – DOM access, mutable refs, forwarding refs
Custom Hooks – extracting and reusing logic
Component Composition – Children, Slots pattern, Compound components
React Router v6 – BrowserRouter, Routes, Route
TanStack Query (React Query) – useQuery, useMutation, useInfiniteQuery
Zustand – store setup, actions, slices, devtools

topics: What is Next.js – SSR, SSG, ISR, CSR explained

generateStaticParams – static generation for dynamic routes

next/image – Image optimization, lazy loading, priority

courses.title: Data Analyst
courses.subtitle: Master Data Analysis – Excel, SQL, Python & Power BI
courses.description: A job-oriented course designed to transform you into a skilled Data Analyst. You will learn to collect, clean, analyze, and visualize data using industry-standard tools like Excel, SQL, Python, and Power BI.
courses.shortDescription: Learn to analyze and visualize data using Excel, SQL, Python & Power BI to drive business decisions.

eligibility.additionalNote: Students from any background – Arts, Commerce, Science can join

modules.title: Introduction to Data Analytics
topics: Types of Data Analytics – Descriptive, Diagnostic, Predictive, Prescriptive
Role and responsibilities of a Data Analyst
Tools overview – Excel, SQL, Python, Power BI

modules.title: Microsoft Excel for Data Analysis
topics: Functions – SUM, AVERAGE, COUNT, IF, VLOOKUP, HLOOKUP, INDEX, MATCH
What-If Analysis – Goal Seek, Data Tables

modules.title: SQL for Data Analysts
topics: Filtering – AND, OR, NOT, BETWEEN, LIKE, IN
Aggregation – COUNT, SUM, AVG, MIN, MAX, GROUP BY, HAVING
Joins – INNER, LEFT, RIGHT, FULL OUTER
Creating and modifying Tables – DDL, DML
Practical: Analyze a real dataset using SQL

modules.title: Python for Data Analysis
topics: Python basics – Variables, Data types, Control flow
Data Cleaning – Handling nulls, duplicates, data types
Matplotlib – Line, Bar, Histogram, Pie charts
Practical: Full EDA on a real dataset

modules.title: Power BI – Data Visualization & Dashboards
topics: Connecting to data sources – Excel, CSV, SQL
DAX Basics – Calculated Columns and Measures
Visualizations – Bar, Line, Map, Card, KPI

modules.title: Capstone Project & Interview Prep
topics: Resume building for Data Analyst roles

instructor.specialization: Business Intelligence, Data Visualization, Python Analytics

learningOutcomes: Perform data cleaning and preparation on real datasets

courses.title: Python Programming
courses.subtitle: Complete Python Programming – Fundamentals to Advanced
courses.description: Master Python — the world's most popular and versatile programming language. This course takes you from absolute zero to advanced Python covering OOP, functional programming, file handling, databases, APIs, automation, web scraping, testing, and real-world projects.
courses.shortDescription: Learn Python from scratch to advanced — OOP, file handling, APIs, automation, databases and real-world projects.

eligibility.additionalNote: Absolute beginners welcome — no prior coding needed

feeStructure.scholarshipDetails: Available for students from low-income families

modules.title: Python Fundamentals
topics: What is Python – history, applications, why Python
IDEs – VS Code, PyCharm, IDLE, Jupyter Notebook
Variables – naming conventions, assignment, multiple assignment
Data Types – int, float, complex, str, bool, NoneType
Type Conversion – int(), float(), str(), bool()
Numeric operations – abs(), round(), pow(), divmod()
print() formatting – sep, end, f-strings, format()
Python keywords and built-in functions overview

modules.title: Control Flow
topics: while loop – syntax and use cases
Iterating strings, lists, tuples, dicts with for
Loop patterns – counting, summing, searching, accumulating

modules.title: Strings – Deep Dive
topics: String creation – single, double, triple quotes
String methods – upper(), lower(), strip(), split(), join(), replace(), find(), count(), startswith(), endswith(), center(), zfill(), encode()
String to list and list to string conversions

modules.title: Data Structures
topics: List methods – append(), extend(), insert(), remove(), pop(), clear(), sort(), reverse(), copy(), count(), index()
Set operations – union, intersection, difference, symmetric difference
Dictionary methods – get(), keys(), values(), items(), update(), pop(), setdefault()
collections module – Counter, defaultdict, OrderedDict, deque, ChainMap
Shallow copy vs Deep copy – copy module

modules.title: Functions & Functional Programming
topics: Defining and calling functions – def keyword
Scope – Local, Enclosing, Global, Built-in (LEGB rule)
Higher-order functions – passing and returning functions
Closures – inner functions and free variables
Recursion examples – factorial, fibonacci, tower of Hanoi

modules.title: Modules, Packages & Virtual Environments
topics: Built-in modules – math, random, statistics, datetime, time, calendar
os module – file system operations, paths, environment variables

modules.title: File Handling
topics: Opening files – open(), file modes (r, w, a, x, b, t, +)
File and directory operations – os, pathlib
CSV files – csv module, reader, writer, DictReader, DictWriter
JSON files – json module, load(), loads(), dump(), dumps()
Logging – logging module, levels, handlers, formatters

modules.title: Exception Handling
topics: What are exceptions vs syntax errors
Common built-in exceptions – ValueError, TypeError, IndexError, KeyError, FileNotFoundError, ZeroDivisionError, AttributeError, ImportError
except Exception as e – accessing error details
Debugging basics – print debugging, pdb module

modules.title: Object-Oriented Programming (OOP)
topics: OOP concepts overview – Encapsulation, Abstraction, Inheritance, Polymorphism
Instance methods, Class methods (@classmethod), Static methods (@staticmethod)
self parameter – what it is and why it exists
Inheritance – single inheritance, inheriting attributes and methods
Multiple Inheritance – Method Resolution Order (MRO), C3 linearization
Abstract classes – abc module, ABC, abstractmethod
Design patterns intro – Singleton, Factory, Observer

modules.title: Advanced Python
topics: Decorators – function decorators, how they work
re functions – match(), search(), findall(), sub(), split(), compile()
Type Hints – typing module, List, Dict, Optional, Union, Tuple, Any
Python 3.10+ type hints – X | Y syntax
f-string advanced – nested expressions, format spec
Memory management – garbage collection, reference counting

modules.title: Working with Databases
topics: SQL basics review – CREATE, INSERT, SELECT, UPDATE, DELETE
SQLite – sqlite3 module, built into Python
SQLite in real projects – CRUD application

modules.title: APIs & Web Scraping
topics: HTTP basics – GET, POST, PUT, DELETE, status codes
GET requests – requests.get(), response, JSON parsing
POST requests – sending data, headers, authentication
Working with REST APIs – OpenWeather, GitHub API
Web Scraping – what it is, legality, robots.txt

modules.title: Testing in Python
topics: unittest – TestCase, setUp, tearDown, assert methods
pytest – setup, writing test functions, fixtures

modules.title: Python Projects
topics: Project planning and structuring a Python project
Project 1 – Student Grade Management System (File + OOP)
Project 2 – Weather App CLI (requests + OpenWeather API)
Project 3 – Expense Tracker (SQLite + OOP + File export)
Project 5 – Task Automation – File Organizer Script
Writing clean, documented, tested Python code
Python coding interview prep – common problems and patterns

learningOutcomes: Write clean, Pythonic, well-structured code
Apply OOP principles in real-world applications
Handle files, JSON, CSV, and databases confidently
Build automation scripts and tools
Work with REST APIs and web scraping
Use concurrency – threading, multiprocessing, asyncio
Write and run tests with pytest
Build a portfolio of 7+ Python projects

Building speed from 0 to 30 WPM in Hindi

Practice with newspaper and official text

courses.title: DSM – Diploma in Software Management
courses.subtitle: Comprehensive Diploma in Computer Applications & Software
courses.description: A one-year diploma program combining hardware fundamentals, software applications, programming, and project work. DSM is a recognized diploma that prepares students for technical and administrative roles in IT companies.
courses.shortDescription: One-year diploma covering computer applications, programming, and software management.

prerequisites: Basic computer knowledge helpful but not mandatory
eligibility.additionalNote: Recognized diploma for government and private sector jobs

Proficiency in MS Office and Tally

Design skills using DTP tools
Job readiness in IT and admin roles

courses.title: CCC – Course on Computer Concepts
courses.subtitle: NIELIT CCC – Nationally Recognized Computer Certificate
courses.description: CCC (Course on Computer Concepts) is a certification by NIELIT (National Institute of Electronics & Information Technology), Government of India. It is mandatory for many government jobs and provides foundational computer literacy.
courses.shortDescription: NIELIT's nationally recognized certificate – mandatory for many government jobs in India.

eligibility.additionalNote: Mandatory for many Rajasthan, UP, MP, and Central Government posts

feeStructure.note: NIELIT exam fee ₹590 is paid directly to NIELIT portal

modules.title: Microsoft Office / LibreOffice
topics: Word Processing – creating and formatting documents

modules.title: Digital Financial Services
topics: Cyber security and safe online practices

examPattern.markingScheme: 1 mark per question, no negative marking

instructor.name: Mr./Ms. [Instructor Name]
instructor.qualification: BCA / Graduate with NIELIT Facilitation Authorization

certification.certificateTitle: CCC – Course on Computer Concepts Certificate
certification.certificateAvailable: From NIELIT official website after result

learningOutcomes: Operate computers with confidence
Use Microsoft Office/LibreOffice for documents and spreadsheets
Use Internet and Email for communication
Perform basic digital financial transactions
Pass the NIELIT CCC online examination
careerOpportunities: Mandatory for many state and central government jobs
Computer literacy certificate for general use

courses.title: BCC – Basic Computer Course
courses.subtitle: NIELIT BCC – Entry-Level Government Recognized Certificate
courses.description: BCC (Basic Computer Course) by NIELIT is an entry-level nationally recognized computer certificate that provides basic digital literacy. It is the stepping stone before CCC and is accepted in various government schemes and rural employment programs.
courses.shortDescription: NIELIT's entry-level government certificate for basic digital literacy – ideal for beginners.

eligibility.additionalNote: Ideal for rural communities, homemakers, and absolute beginners

feeStructure.note: Very affordable – targeted at grassroots digital literacy

learningOutcomes: Use a computer for the first time
Create simple documents
Browse the internet and use email
Get a government-recognized computer certificate
careerOpportunities: Digital literacy for rural employment schemes

courses.title: CIT – Certificate in Information Technology
courses.subtitle: Comprehensive Certificate in IT Skills
courses.description: CIT (Certificate in Information Technology) is a 6-month comprehensive certificate program covering computer fundamentals, office applications, internet, programming basics, and graphic design. It is widely recognized by employers for IT entry-level roles.
courses.shortDescription: 6-month comprehensive IT certificate – computer skills, applications, design, and programming basics.

eligibility.additionalNote: Perfect for students who want a broader IT knowledge base

feeStructure.scholarshipDetails: Scholarship for SC/ST/OBC students – up to 15% off

Work safely and productively online
